summary.intent: Install and run the Gossamer VS Code extension, fixing a SQLite database initialization error that appeared when starting the server.
summary.outcome: Identified two root causes: (1) schema.sql was missing from the VSIX due to a stale build using an older script version, and (2) Prisma's upgrade nag banner was captured into schema.sql and caused a SQLite syntax error. Both were fixed and the VSIX was rebuilt successfully.

repo: The VSIX is built via scripts/package-vsix.sh, which copies packages/core/prisma/schema.sql into bundled-server/ before packaging.

pushSchema() reads schema.sql from SCRIPT_DIR (resolved via import.meta.url) and strips only '--' prefixed comment lines before executing as raw SQL — it does not handle non-SQL output like Prisma CLI banners.

In dev/debug mode, GossamerPanel.ts prefers BUNDLED_SERVE (bundled-server/serve.js) over DEV_SERVE if the bundled-server directory exists, meaning debug sessions use the VSIX-bundled server, not the source.
Schema is pushed per-repo: at server startup for the current repo's DB (server.ts:168), and again during repo onboarding (server.ts:1230). It does not migrate all tracked repos at startup.
The Prisma CLI upgrade nag banner (box-drawing characters) can be captured into schema.sql if prisma migrate diff output is redirected to file without filtering stderr/stdout noise.

code.finding: pushSchema() called at startup for the current repo's DB path only.
pushSchema() called during repo registration/onboarding for each new repo's DB.
setupLogContentFts() runs after pushSchema() — if pushSchema fails silently, LogContent table won't exist and this will throw.
BUNDLED_SERVE is preferred over DEV_SERVE when bundled-server/ directory is present, even in debug mode.
Prisma upgrade nag banner (┌── Update available 7.4.2 -> 7.7.0 ──┐) was appended to the file at generation time and caused SQLite syntax errors.
Step that copies schema.sql from packages/core/prisma/schema.sql into bundled-server/ — was present in script but not executed in the stale prior build.

workflow: vsce respects .gitignore when packaging — files in git-ignored directories won't be included in the VSIX even if physically present.

Stale build directories (bundled-server/) from prior runs can survive if the clean step (rm -rf) doesn't execute, leading to subtle packaging bugs where new build steps appear to work but old artifacts are shipped.
Prisma CLI may print update/upgrade banners to stdout; when capturing migrate diff output to a .sql file, this output should be filtered or stderr/stdout separated to avoid corrupting the SQL file.

friction: Build appeared to succeed but shipped a stale bundled-server/ because a prior run's artifacts survived — no visible error during packaging.
pushSchema() silently swallows or propagates errors without clearly indicating whether schema.sql was found and read successfully, making diagnosis harder.
Debug extension host uses the bundled server instead of the dev source, creating confusing parity issues between what's being debugged and the actual source.
Prisma CLI upgrade nag banner contaminated schema.sql silently — no validation step checked that the generated SQL was syntactically valid before committing or shipping.
open_items: pushSchema() only strips '--' comment lines; consider stripping all non-SQL content or validating SQL before executing to guard against future Prisma CLI output contamination.
The schema.sql generation process should filter/separate Prisma CLI stdout from the actual SQL output to prevent banner recurrence.
Consider adding a schema.sql syntax validation step to the package-vsix.sh build script.
Dev mode should ideally use the source serve.ts/dist/serve.js instead of bundled-server to avoid debug/source parity issues.
